name: Blockchair
description: Free universal blockchain explorer supporting Bitcoin, Ethereum, and 40+ chains. Search transactions, addresses, and blocks. Useful for tracing crypto payments in trafficking and CSAM distribution networks.
input: Wallet address, transaction hash, block number
output: Transaction history, wallet balance, linked addresses
legalNotes: Public blockchain data. No warrant required for on-chain data. Evidence should be corroborated.

name: OXT (Bitcoin Graph Tool)
description: Free Bitcoin transaction graph explorer. Visualises transaction flows between wallets, helping investigators trace crypto funds through mixing and hopping across exploitation payment networks.
output: Visual transaction graph showing fund flows

name: Breadcrumbs
description: Free crypto investigation tool with visual wallet graph analysis. Traces fund flows across wallets, flags high-risk addresses, and generates shareable investigation reports. Used by investigators to follow exploitation payment trails.
output: Visual fund flow graph, risk scoring, report export
legalNotes: Public blockchain data. Free tier available for investigators.

name: Arkham Intelligence
description: Free on-chain entity tracking platform that labels wallets to real-world identities (exchanges, protocols, known criminals). Tracks funds across chains and generates alerts when flagged wallets transact.
output: Entity labels, transaction history, cross-chain tracking
legalNotes: Free tier available. Paid plans for advanced features.

name: Etherscan
description: The primary free Ethereum blockchain explorer. Search any Ethereum address, transaction, or smart contract. Essential for tracing ETH and ERC-20 token payments in exploitation networks.
input: Ethereum address, transaction hash, token contract
output: Full transaction history, token transfers, contract interactions

name: GraphSense
description: Open source cryptoasset analytics platform built for investigators. Full data sovereignty — run on your own infrastructure. Supports Bitcoin, Ethereum, Litecoin. Used by law enforcement agencies in Europe for exploitation finance investigations.
output: Cluster analysis, entity attribution, transaction graph
legalNotes: Open source. Self-hosted. Data stays on your infrastructure — important for sensitive LE investigations.

name: Chainalysis Reactor
description: Industry-leading blockchain investigation platform used by law enforcement in 70+ countries. Traces crypto across 27+ blockchains, links wallets to real-world entities, and produces court-ready evidence reports. Used in major CSAM and trafficking prosecutions.
output: Entity attribution, fund flow graph, court-ready reports
legalNotes: Enterprise pricing ~$40,000+/year. Law enforcement agencies may qualify for subsidised access. Contact Chainalysis public sector team.
evidenceAdmissibility: Chainalysis reports are accepted as evidence in courts globally.

name: TRM Labs
description: Blockchain intelligence platform screening 45+ blockchains and 200M+ digital assets. Used by financial institutions and law enforcement to investigate exploitation finance and money laundering. Free access available for government/LE agencies.
output: Risk scoring, entity labels, cross-chain tracing
legalNotes: Government and law enforcement agencies may qualify for free access. Contact TRM public sector team.

name: Ghana Police Cyber Crime Unit
description: Ghana Police Service Cyber Crime Unit with a dedicated Child Protection Digital Forensics Laboratory. Investigates online child exploitation and abuse. Supported by UNICEF and NCMEC. Decentralised across all 25 regional police commands in Ghana.
legalNotes: Ghana jurisdiction. For cross-border cases coordinate via INTERPOL NCB Accra.

name: AFRIPOL Cybercrime Reporting
description: African Union's police cooperation mechanism for cybercrime. Coordinates joint operations across 55 AU member states including child exploitation cases. Works with INTERPOL's AFJOC programme to share intelligence and deploy forensic tools across Africa.
input: Case referral via national police liaison
output: Cross-border intelligence sharing, joint investigation support
legalNotes: Access via national police AFRIPOL liaison officer. Not for direct public use.

name: AFJOC (African Joint Operation Against Cybercrime)
description: INTERPOL-led joint operation programme providing African law enforcement agencies with cybercrime investigation equipment, blockchain analysis software, and digital forensic capabilities. Has distributed tools to 36 African countries including Ghana.
output: Technical assistance, tool access, joint investigation support
legalNotes: Law enforcement only via INTERPOL National Central Bureau.

name: Ahmia
description: Free search engine for Tor hidden services (.onion sites). Indexes publicly accessible dark web content allowing investigators to search for exploitation-related terms without directly browsing Tor. Available on both clearweb and as a .onion site.
output: List of .onion sites matching the query
legalNotes: Use with caution — searching for CSAM-related terms may trigger alerts. Use through official investigative channels only.

name: OnionScan
description: Open source tool for investigating and scanning Tor hidden services (.onion sites). Identifies misconfigurations that can deanonymise operators — useful for locating administrators of CSAM or trafficking sites.
output: Configuration analysis, potential deanonymisation data, metadata
legalNotes: Requires Tor. Actively scans target — ensure legal authority before scanning.

name: DeepDarkCTI
description: Collection of cyber threat intelligence sources from the deep and dark web. Provides researchers and investigators with curated links to dark web forums, markets, and leak sites relevant to exploitation investigations.
legalNotes: Use through secure, isolated investigation environment. Ensure legal authority.
